label: NIH — National Institutes of Health (investigator-initiated Research Project Grants)
version: NIH Simplified Peer Review Framework, effective for due dates on/after 25 Jan 2025 (NOT-OD-24-010)

sections.title: Specific Aims
sections.purpose: One page that sells the whole application; reviewers often score from here.
mustContain: Hook / Significance opening (4-6 sentences): compelling, jargon-light first sentence; big problem -> what is known -> the specific gap
Knowledge gap + critical need + falsifiable central hypothesis (with the why, grounded in preliminary data/literature)
Long-term goal and the objective of this application
2-3 aims (rarely 4), each a bolded action title + 2-4 sentences giving working hypothesis, approach, and what the aim yields
Aims that are hypothesis-driven, distinct, and non-interdependent (Aim 1 failing must not sink Aims 2-3)
Payoff/Impact paragraph: expected outcomes, how they move the field, alignment with the assigned IC mission

sections.title: Research Strategy — Significance
sections.purpose: Establish why the work matters (feeds Factor 1).
mustContain: Scientific premise: the prior work the proposal rests on, with honest strengths AND weaknesses
The problem/gap and why it is important
Expected contribution of the project
Tie to the assigned IC's mission

sections.title: Research Strategy — Innovation
sections.purpose: State what is new or paradigm-shifting (feeds Factor 1).
mustContain: Novel concept/model/method/intervention/application
Distinguish 'novel to the field' from 'new to me'
Avoid overclaiming incremental work as transformative

sections.title: Research Strategy — Approach
sections.purpose: The highest-stakes section (feeds Factor 2; strongest correlation with overall impact).
mustContain: Organized by Specific Aim
Per aim: rationale/hypothesis -> experimental design -> methods -> expected outcomes -> interpretation of possible results
Potential problems, alternative approaches, and contingencies for each aim (mandatory)
Benchmarks/feasibility and a realistic timeline
Preliminary data supporting feasibility (effectively expected for R01)
Rigor: controls, sample size / power analysis / statistics, randomization/blinding

sections.title: Authentication of Key Biological and/or Chemical Resources
sections.purpose: Separate attachment (outside the page-limited Strategy) documenting authentication.
mustContain: Methods to authenticate each key biological/chemical resource
Submitted as a separate attachment, not within the Research Strategy page limit

sections.title: Biosketch
mustContain: Personal Statement tailored to THIS project (address any gaps)
Positions/Honors
Contributions to Science (up to 5 short narratives, each with up to ~4 citations)
Research Support
Correct current format for the due date (legacy 5-page vs NIH Common Form per-section character limits)

sections.title: Facilities & Other Resources
sections.purpose: Show how the environment specifically enables this project (feeds Factor 3).
mustContain: Project-specific description of facilities/environment, not boilerplate
Explicit link from resources to the proposed work

sections.title: Equipment
sections.purpose: List equipment available for the project.
mustContain: Equipment relevant to executing the aims

sections.title: Budget & Justification
sections.purpose: Modular or detailed budget with narrative; an Additional Review Consideration (does not change score).
mustContain: Modular (typical R01, <= $250K direct/yr in $25K modules) or detailed budget
Justification narrative consistent with the proposed work

sections.title: Data Management & Sharing (DMS) Plan
sections.purpose: Required when the project generates scientific data.
mustContain: How data will be managed and shared (if generating data)

sections.title: Other Plans as Applicable
sections.purpose: Compliance and team attachments that apply to the specific project.
mustContain: Human Subjects & Clinical Trials information
Inclusion (across the lifespan / sex/gender / race-ethnicity) where applicable
Vertebrate Animals Section (VAS) where applicable
Multiple-PI Leadership Plan (if multi-PI)
Letters of Support
Resource Sharing

limits.font: Arial, Helvetica, Palatino Linotype, or Georgia >= 11pt; margins >= 0.5in

reviewCriteria: Factor 1 — Importance of the Research (absorbs Significance + Innovation; scored 1-9; 'Should it be done?')
Factor 2 — Rigor and Feasibility (absorbs Approach; scored 1-9; highest correlation with overall impact; 'Can it be done well?')
Factor 3 — Expertise and Resources (absorbs Investigator + Environment; binary appropriate vs additional-needed, comment only if a gap; 'Will it be done?')
Overall Impact Score: 1 (best) - 9 (worst) per reviewer -> averaged x10 -> 10-90 -> percentiled vs the IC-specific payline (~50% triaged / Not Discussed)
Additional Review Criteria (affect score where applicable): Protections for Human Subjects, Vertebrate Animals, Biohazards, Resubmission/Renewal/Revision
Additional Review Considerations (assessed, do not change score): budget/period, inclusion/authentication/resource-sharing

checklist.check: Specific Aims fits within one page.
Research Strategy is within the page limit (12 pages R01 / 6 pages R21).
All required sections present: Specific Aims, Significance, Innovation, Approach, and Authentication of Key Resources attachment.
Authentication of Key Biological/Chemical Resources is submitted as a separate attachment (not inside the Research Strategy).
Every citation resolves to a real DOI/PMID.
Aims are non-interdependent — if Aim 1 fails, Aims 2-3 still yield valuable results (no 'house of cards').
There are 2-3 aims (rarely 4), each with a bolded action title.
Aims are hypothesis-driven, not merely descriptive ('characterize X') — no fishing expedition.
A falsifiable central hypothesis plus the gap and critical need are clear by the end of paragraph 2 of Specific Aims.
Payoff/Impact is explicit and tied to the assigned IC's mission.
Significance states the scientific premise with honest strengths AND weaknesses.
Innovation is specific and not overstated; incremental work is not dressed as transformative.
Approach is organized by aim with design, methods, expected results, and interpretation of possible results.
Potential problems, alternative approaches, and contingencies are present for each aim.
Preliminary data supports feasibility (effectively required for R01; not required for R21).
Rigor elements present: power/sample-size justification, statistics, controls, randomization/blinding.
Sex As a Biological Variable (SABV) is addressed, or a strong single-sex justification is given.
Font, font size, and margins meet NIH formatting rules (>= 11pt approved font, margins >= 0.5in).
Biosketch uses the correct current format for the due date (legacy 5-page vs Common Form) with a personal statement tailored to this project.
For a resubmission, the Introduction responds point-by-point to the prior summary statement.

redFlags: Overly ambitious / not feasible in the time, budget, or team — the #1 Approach killer
Interdependent aims (a 'house of cards' where later aims depend on earlier results)
No pitfalls, alternatives, or contingency plans — reads as naive
Descriptive, not hypothesis-driven aims; a 'fishing expedition'
Thin scientific premise; overstated significance; incremental work dressed as innovative
Missing rigor elements — no power/sample-size, no controls, SABV not addressed, no authentication
Mismatch of team/environment to the project (now a Factor 3 binary flag)
Poor writing/organization — dense text, undefined jargon, unsupportive figures, typos, over-length
Lack of overall reviewer enthusiasm -> triage (Not Discussed)
Resubmissions that do not visibly respond to the prior summary statement
requiredAttachments: Research Strategy (12 pages R01 / 6 pages R21)
Authentication of Key Biological and/or Chemical Resources
Data Management & Sharing (DMS) Plan (if generating data)
Human Subjects & Clinical Trials information (if applicable)
recencyNote: Verify per cycle: (1) the exact Biosketch format required for the due date (legacy 5-page vs NIH Common Form per-section character limits, currently in transition); (2) any post-2025 tweaks to the three-Factor Simplified Review Framework (current as of the 25 Jan 2025 rollout); (3) the IC-specific payline and whether the target IC accepts the Parent R21; (4) confirm all page limits, fonts, and current forms against the live NOFO/RFA.
